results.question: At the 2025 Belgian GP, compare each top-team's first-stint inter-pace delta to leader and pinpoint who waited too long to switch to slicks — was Hamilton's early switch optimal vs Verstappen's later one?
results.author_note: F1.com Belgian GP report: Piastri won wet-dry Spa, Hamilton early to slicks finished 6th, Verstappen 4th.
results.adequacyReason: Request failed before a usable answer was produced.
results.answer: I couldn't construct a valid SQL query for this question. The query referenced columns that don't exist on the targeted contract: le.normalized_compound (no such column on core.laps_enriched).
results.generationNotes: This query analyzes the 2025 Belgian GP (session_key=9939) focusing on Hamilton (44) and Verstappen (1) to compare their intermediate tyre strategy. It: (1) computes per-lap delta to the session leader for all valid laps; (2) isolates inter-tyre laps for both drivers and calculates average/best/worst delta to leader on inters; (3) examines the last 3 inter laps to detect pace deterioration trend before the switch; (4) identifies when each driver switched to slicks and their first slick lap performance; (5) applies a heuristic assessment of whether the switch timing was optimal based on whether the final inter laps showed significant pace degradation vs their overall inter average. This surfaces whether Hamilton's earlier switch was proactive/optimal or premature, and whether Verstappen's later switch cost him time by staying on deteriorating inters. | The only fix needed was removing the reference to `le.normalized_compound` which does not exist in `core.laps_enriched`. That column was selected in the `all_laps` CTE and propagated to `focal_laps` and `inter_lap_deltas`. Since `normalized_compound` is not in the available columns list for `core.laps_enriched` (the correct column is `compound_name`), I simply removed all references to `normalized_compound` throughout the query. The `compound_name` column already serves the same filtering purpose (ILIKE '%INTER%' checks) and is retained. All other logic — inter stint identification, slick switch timing, delta-to-leader calculations, and the switch timing assessment — remains intact. | auto_repair_applied | sql_repair_timeout
results.sql:
WITH session_drivers AS (
  SELECT sd.driver_number, sd.full_name, sd.team_name
  FROM core.session_drivers sd
  WHERE sd.session_key = 9939
    AND sd.driver_number IN (1, 44)
),

-- All laps for session to find leader pace per lap
all_laps AS (
  SELECT
    le.lap_number,
    le.driver_number,
    le.driver_name,
    le.team_name,
    le.lap_duration,
    le.compound_name,
    le.normalized_compound,
    le.is_slick,
    le.stint_number,
    le.tyre_age_on_lap,
    le.is_pit_out_lap,
    le.is_pit_lap,
    le.is_valid,
    le.position_end_of_lap
  FROM core.laps_enriched le
  WHERE le.session_key = 9939
    AND le.is_valid = TRUE
    AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
    AND le.lap_duration > 0
),

-- Leader pace per lap (min valid lap time across all drivers)
leader_pace AS (
  SELECT
    lap_number,
    MIN(lap_duration) AS leader_lap_time
  FROM all_laps
  GROUP BY lap_number
),

-- Focus on VER (1) and HAM (44) laps with leader delta
focal_laps AS (
  SELECT
    al.lap_number,
    al.driver_number,
    al.driver_name,
    al.team_name,
    al.lap_duration,
    al.compound_name,
    al.normalized_compound,
    al.is_slick,
    al.stint_number,
    al.tyre_age_on_lap,
    al.is_pit_out_lap,
    al.is_pit_lap,
    al.position_end_of_lap,
    lp.leader_lap_time,
    ROUND((al.lap_duration - lp.leader_lap_time)::numeric, 3) AS delta_to_leader_s
  FROM all_laps al
  JOIN leader_pace lp ON al.lap_number = lp.lap_number
  WHERE al.driver_number IN (1, 44)
),

-- Stint summary for VER and HAM
stint_info AS (
  SELECT
    ss.driver_number,
    ss.driver_name,
    ss.team_name,
    ss.stint_number,
    ss.compound_name,
    ss.lap_start,
    ss.lap_end,
    ss.stint_length_laps,
    ss.avg_valid_lap,
    ss.best_valid_lap,
    ss.degradation_per_lap,
    ss.tyre_age_at_start,
    ss.fresh_tyre
  FROM core.stint_summary ss
  WHERE ss.session_key = 9939
    AND ss.driver_number IN (1, 44)
),

-- Identify inter stints (first stint on inters)
inter_stints AS (
  SELECT
    si.*,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY si.driver_number ORDER BY si.lap_start) AS inter_stint_rank
  FROM stint_info si
  WHERE si.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%'
),

-- Identify slick stints (first switch to slicks)
slick_stints AS (
  SELECT
    si.*,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY si.driver_number ORDER BY si.lap_start) AS slick_stint_rank
  FROM stint_info si
  WHERE si.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
    AND si.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%WET%'
),

-- First inter stint per driver
first_inter AS (
  SELECT * FROM inter_stints WHERE inter_stint_rank = 1
),

-- First slick stint per driver
first_slick AS (
  SELECT * FROM slick_stints WHERE slick_stint_rank = 1
),

-- Per-lap inter pace delta for VER and HAM
inter_lap_deltas AS (
  SELECT
    fl.driver_number,
    fl.driver_name,
    fl.team_name,
    fl.lap_number,
    fl.compound_name,
    fl.is_slick,
    fl.lap_duration,
    fl.leader_lap_time,
    fl.delta_to_leader_s,
    fl.tyre_age_on_lap,
    fl.position_end_of_lap,
    fl.stint_number
  FROM focal_laps fl
  WHERE fl.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%'
),

-- Average inter-stint delta to leader per driver
inter_avg_delta AS (
  SELECT
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    team_name,
    COUNT(*) AS inter_laps_on_inters,
    ROUND(AVG(delta_to_leader_s)::numeric, 3) AS avg_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
    ROUND(MIN(delta_to_leader_s)::numeric, 3) AS best_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
    ROUND(MAX(delta_to_leader_s)::numeric, 3) AS worst_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s
  FROM inter_lap_deltas
  GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, team_name
),

-- Last 3 inter laps delta trend (to detect if pace was deteriorating before switch)
last_inter_laps AS (
  SELECT
    ild.driver_number,
    ild.driver_name,
    ROUND(AVG(ild.delta_to_leader_s)::numeric, 3) AS avg_delta_last3_inter_laps_s,
    MAX(ild.lap_number) AS last_inter_lap
  FROM (
    SELECT *,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number ORDER BY lap_number DESC) AS rn
    FROM inter_lap_deltas
  ) ild
  WHERE ild.rn <= 3
  GROUP BY ild.driver_number, ild.driver_name
),

-- First slick lap delta to leader (how quickly they got up to speed)
first_slick_lap_delta AS (
  SELECT
    fl.driver_number,
    fl.driver_name,
    fl.lap_number AS first_slick_lap,
    fl.lap_duration AS first_slick_lap_time_s,
    fl.leader_lap_time AS leader_time_at_slick_switch_s,
    fl.delta_to_leader_s AS first_slick_delta_to_leader_s,
    fl.compound_name AS slick_compound
  FROM focal_laps fl
  JOIN first_slick fs ON fl.driver_number = fs.driver_number
    AND fl.lap_number = fs.lap_start
  WHERE fl.is_pit_out_lap = FALSE OR fl.is_pit_out_lap IS NULL
),

-- Combine everything
final_summary AS (
  SELECT
    sd.driver_number,
    sd.full_name AS driver_name,
    sd.team_name,
    -- Inter stint info
    fi.lap_start AS inter_stint_start_lap,
    fi.lap_end AS inter_stint_end_lap,
    fi.stint_length_laps AS inter_stint_length_laps,
    fi.avg_valid_lap AS inter_avg_lap_s,
    fi.degradation_per_lap AS inter_deg_per_lap_s,
    -- Slick switch info
    fs.lap_start AS slick_switch_lap,
    fs.compound_name AS slick_compound,
    -- Pace deltas on inters
    iad.inter_laps_on_inters,
    iad.avg_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
    iad.best_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
    iad.worst_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
    -- Last 3 inter laps trend
    lil.avg_delta_last3_inter_laps_s,
    lil.last_inter_lap,
    -- First slick lap performance
    fsd.first_slick_lap,
    fsd.first_slick_lap_time_s,
    fsd.first_slick_delta_to_leader_s,
    -- Derived: laps stayed on inters after pace started deteriorating
    (lil.last_inter_lap - fi.lap_start + 1) AS total_inter_laps,
    CASE
      WHEN iad.avg_delta_last3_inter_laps_s > iad.avg_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s + 1.5
        THEN 'Likely waited too long — pace deteriorated significantly in final inter laps'
      WHEN iad.avg_delta_last3_inter_laps_s > iad.avg_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s + 0.5
        THEN 'Marginal — slight pace drop in final inter laps'
      ELSE 'Switched at reasonable time — inter pace held'
    END AS switch_timing_assessment
  FROM session_drivers sd
  LEFT JOIN first_inter fi ON sd.driver_number = fi.driver_number
  LEFT JOIN first_slick fs ON sd.driver_number = fs.driver_number
  LEFT JOIN inter_avg_delta iad ON sd.driver_number = iad.driver_number
  LEFT JOIN last_inter_laps lil ON sd.driver_number = lil.driver_number
  LEFT JOIN first_slick_lap_delta fsd ON sd.driver_number = fsd.driver_number
)

SELECT
  driver_number,
  driver_name,
  team_name,
  inter_stint_start_lap,
  inter_stint_end_lap,
  inter_stint_length_laps,
  ROUND(inter_avg_lap_s::numeric, 3) AS inter_avg_lap_s,
  ROUND(inter_deg_per_lap_s::numeric, 3) AS inter_deg_per_lap_s,
  slick_switch_lap,
  slick_compound,
  inter_laps_on_inters,
  avg_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
  best_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
  worst_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
  avg_delta_last3_inter_laps_s,
  last_inter_lap,
  first_slick_lap,
  ROUND(first_slick_lap_time_s::numeric, 3) AS first_slick_lap_time_s,
  ROUND(first_slick_delta_to_leader_s::numeric, 3) AS first_slick_delta_to_leader_s,
  switch_timing_assessment
FROM final_summary
ORDER BY slick_switch_lap ASC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 20

results.question: For the 2025 British GP, identify the inters-to-slicks (and slicks-to-inters) crossover-lap windows during each rain band and quantify the position swing for any driver who pitted off-window.
results.author_note: F1.com / Mercedes / McLaren British GP reports: 5 drivers gambled on slicks at start; Hülkenberg's maiden podium.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: For the 2025 British GP, the data identifies two main tyre-crossover windows: a slick-to-inters window around lap 10–11 (triggered by rain) and an inters-to-slicks window that varied by driver roughly between laps 38–45 as the track dried. The vast majority of drivers pitted on-window (laps_off_window = 0), including Verstappen (inters→medium lap 42), Norris (inters→medium lap 45), Gasly (inters→medium lap 42), Leclerc (inters→soft lap 43), Antonelli (hard→inters lap 10), and Stroll's first stop (soft→inters lap 11). The one notable off-window case is Lance Stroll's inters-to-slicks transition on lap 42, which the data flags as 35 laps LATE relative to his driver-specific inter-to-slick crossover lap of 7 — however, this crossover lap of 7 appears to reflect an earlier dry window before the rain band, not the post-rain drying phase, so the 35-lap offset is likely a data artefact rather than a true strategic error. No driver in the returned rows shows a measurable position swing attributable to an off-window pit stop, as position data around Stroll's late stop is null for the lap before the pit.
results.answerReasoning: The rows show all transition types (SLICK_TO_INTER and INTER_TO_SLICK), their pit laps, crossover laps, laps_off_window values, and position before/after pit. Nearly every driver has laps_off_window = 0 (ON_WINDOW). Stroll's stint 4 shows laps_off_window = 35 (LATE) but his inter_to_slick_crossover_lap is 7, which predates the rain band entirely, making the offset misleading. Position swing data is null for Stroll's late stop, so no quantifiable position swing can be confirmed. All other drivers with position data show 0 or +1 position swing, none attributable to off-window timing.
results.generationNotes:
The query has four logical stages:

1. **crossover_windows** – pulls each driver's `slick_to_inter_crossover_lap`, `inter_to_slick_crossover_lap`, and combined `crossover_lap` from `analytics.weather_impact` for session 9947. Because the crossover lap is repeated on every row, a GROUP BY collapses it to one row per driver.

2. **pit_events** – reads `core.stint_summary` for session 9947, restricting to stints > 1 (i.e. post-pit stints) and using `LAG` to capture the previous compound so we can classify each transition (SLICK→INTER, INTER→SLICK, WET→INTER, etc.).

3. **pits_with_crossover** – joins pit events to crossover windows and computes `laps_off_window` (positive = late, negative = early) relative to the appropriate crossover lap for each transition type.

4. **pit_position_context** – joins to `core.race_progression_summary` to fetch the driver's position one lap before and one lap after the pit stop, computing the raw position swing and a GAINED/LOST/NEUTRAL verdict.

The final SELECT surfaces only wet-compound transitions (INTER↔SLICK, WET↔INTER) with their timing verdict (ON_WINDOW / EARLY / LATE) and the resulting position swing, ordered by driver and pit lap. The context pins session_key = 9947 (2025 British GP) and driver_number = 1, but the query is written to cover all drivers in the session so the full field picture is visible. | The original error was that `pe.transition_type` was referenced in the WHERE clause of the `pits_with_crossover` CTE, but `transition_type` is a computed CASE alias within that same CTE — PostgreSQL does not allow referencing a SELECT-list alias in the same query's WHERE clause. The fix moves the transition_type filter into the next CTE (`pit_position_context`) WHERE clause, where `pwc.transition_type` is a real column from the already-materialized `pits_with_crossover` CTE. All other logic remains intact. | auto_repair_applied | session_pin_verified(session_key=9947)
results.sql:
WITH crossover_windows AS (
  SELECT
    wi.driver_number,
    wi.driver_name,
    wi.team_name,
    wi.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
    wi.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
    wi.crossover_lap
  FROM analytics.weather_impact wi
  WHERE wi.session_key = 9947
  GROUP BY
    wi.driver_number,
    wi.driver_name,
    wi.team_name,
    wi.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
    wi.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
    wi.crossover_lap
),
pit_events AS (
  SELECT
    ss.driver_number,
    ss.driver_name,
    ss.team_name,
    ss.stint_number,
    ss.compound_name,
    ss.lap_start        AS pit_in_lap,
    ss.tyre_age_at_start,
    LAG(ss.compound_name) OVER (
      PARTITION BY ss.driver_number ORDER BY ss.stint_number
    ) AS prev_compound
  FROM core.stint_summary ss
  WHERE ss.session_key = 9947
    AND ss.stint_number > 1
),
pits_with_crossover AS (
  SELECT
    pe.driver_number,
    pe.driver_name,
    pe.team_name,
    pe.stint_number,
    pe.prev_compound,
    pe.compound_name      AS new_compound,
    pe.pit_in_lap,
    cw.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
    cw.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
    cw.crossover_lap,
    CASE
      WHEN pe.prev_compound ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%WET%'
        THEN 'INTER_TO_SLICK'
      WHEN pe.prev_compound NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.prev_compound NOT ILIKE '%WET%'
           AND pe.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%'
        THEN 'SLICK_TO_INTER'
      WHEN pe.prev_compound ILIKE '%WET%'
           AND pe.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%'
        THEN 'WET_TO_INTER'
      WHEN pe.prev_compound ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.compound_name ILIKE '%WET%'
        THEN 'INTER_TO_WET'
      ELSE 'OTHER'
    END AS transition_type,
    CASE
      WHEN pe.prev_compound ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%WET%'
        THEN pe.pit_in_lap - cw.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap
      WHEN pe.prev_compound NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
           AND pe.prev_compound NOT ILIKE '%WET%'
           AND pe.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%'
        THEN pe.pit_in_lap - cw.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap
      ELSE NULL
    END AS laps_off_window
  FROM pit_events pe
  LEFT JOIN crossover_windows cw
    ON pe.driver_number = cw.driver_number
  WHERE pe.prev_compound IS NOT NULL
),
position_swing AS (
  SELECT
    rp.driver_number,
    rp.lap_number,
    rp.position_end_of_lap,
    rp.previous_position,
    rp.positions_gained_this_lap
  FROM core.race_progression_summary rp
  WHERE rp.session_key = 9947
),
pit_position_context AS (
  SELECT
    pwc.*,
    pre_pit.position_end_of_lap  AS position_before_pit,
    post_pit.position_end_of_lap AS position_after_pit,
    post_pit.position_end_of_lap - pre_pit.position_end_of_lap AS position_swing
  FROM pits_with_crossover pwc
  LEFT JOIN position_swing pre_pit
    ON pre_pit.driver_number = pwc.driver_number
   AND pre_pit.lap_number    = pwc.pit_in_lap - 1
  LEFT JOIN position_swing post_pit
    ON post_pit.driver_number = pwc.driver_number
   AND post_pit.lap_number    = pwc.pit_in_lap + 1
  WHERE pwc.transition_type IN ('INTER_TO_SLICK','SLICK_TO_INTER','WET_TO_INTER','INTER_TO_WET')
)
SELECT
  ppc.driver_number,
  ppc.driver_name,
  ppc.team_name,
  ppc.stint_number,
  ppc.prev_compound,
  ppc.new_compound,
  ppc.transition_type,
  ppc.pit_in_lap,
  ppc.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
  ppc.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
  ppc.crossover_lap                                          AS combined_crossover_lap,
  ppc.laps_off_window,
  CASE
    WHEN ppc.laps_off_window IS NULL THEN 'N/A'
    WHEN ABS(ppc.laps_off_window) <= 1 THEN 'ON_WINDOW'
    WHEN ppc.laps_off_window > 1      THEN 'LATE'
    WHEN ppc.laps_off_window < -1     THEN 'EARLY'
  END                                                        AS timing_verdict,
  ppc.position_before_pit,
  ppc.position_after_pit,
  ppc.position_swing,
  CASE
    WHEN ppc.position_swing < 0 THEN 'GAINED'
    WHEN ppc.position_swing > 0 THEN 'LOST'
    ELSE 'NEUTRAL'
  END                                                        AS swing_direction
FROM pit_position_context ppc
ORDER BY ppc.driver_number, ppc.pit_in_lap
LIMIT 200
